# Latest News
[ ![HQS launches HQSpectrum — full release of its NMR spectrum prediction and analysis software](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1772214715330-9RPUM4XSK8KRHWNH899N/Folie3.PNG?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqspectrum-full-release)
February 27, 2026
[ HQS launches HQSpectrum — full release of its NMR spectrum prediction and analysis software ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqspectrum-full-release)
February 27, 2026
HQS Quantum Simulations introduces HQSpectrum, the first full version of its software for predicting and analyzing Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectra, designed to reduce manual effort in NMR analysis and to improve traceability. Users can preprocess experimental spectra, compare them against computed spectra, adjust results interactively, and export documentation-ready reports.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqspectrum-full-release)
February 27, 2026
[ ![Year in Review 2025: Accelerating Quantum‑Powered Spectroscopy at HQS](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1765796155060-SAUCWQ0RWNDH367I2Y5F/review.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/annual-review-2025)
December 15, 2025
[ Year in Review 2025: Accelerating Quantum‑Powered Spectroscopy at HQS ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/annual-review-2025)
December 15, 2025
2025 got off to a spectacular start for HQS Quantum Simulations: the year began with our EIC Transition success, setting the tone for twelve months focused on scaling impact, expanding ready‑to‑use workflows, and strengthening collaborations that bring quantum methods for spectroscopy closer to real‑world use.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/annual-review-2025)
December 15, 2025
[ ![Dr Nicolas Vogt Appointed Chief Innovation Officer at HQS ](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1765398233552-SMD4DLUKJTL8NYDTEYIV/Nicolas-Vogt_1-1_news_hqs.jpg?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/dr-nicolas-vogt-appointed-chief-innovation-officer-at-hqs)
December 11, 2025
[ Dr Nicolas Vogt Appointed Chief Innovation Officer at HQS ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/dr-nicolas-vogt-appointed-chief-innovation-officer-at-hqs)
December 11, 2025
HQS Quantum Simulations is pleased to announce that its management team has grown. Dr Nicolas Vogt, previously Head of Quantum Computing, has been appointed Chief Innovation Officer.   
In his new role, Dr Vogt will focus on exploring new application areas and developing prototypes that will later be integrated into the HQSpectrum product line. 
The position is designed to sit at the intersection of cutting edge research and product development, ensuring that novel concepts in spectroscopy and quantum technologies are translated quickly into solutions for customers. 
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/dr-nicolas-vogt-appointed-chief-innovation-officer-at-hqs)
December 11, 2025
[ ![HQS releases Electron Spectroscopy Use Cases](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1762940425774-9D4G4WOKA7J1MV91DC3V/picture+news.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-releases-electron-spectroscopy-use-cases)
November 12, 2025
[ HQS releases Electron Spectroscopy Use Cases ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-releases-electron-spectroscopy-use-cases)
November 12, 2025
Quantum simulation is rapidly reshaping electron spectroscopy. By mapping complex many-body problems onto compact quantum models, researchers can estimate excitation energies and singlet–triplet gaps using smaller circuit footprints, bringing previously computationally challenging observables within the reach of today's and tomorrow's quantum hardware.
HQS Quantum Simulations is making new Electron Spectroscopy use cases available on the HQS Cloud together with a complimentary 6‑month license of the HQStage module HQS Quantum Solver for evaluation.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-releases-electron-spectroscopy-use-cases)
November 12, 2025
[ ![HQS is XPRIZE Quantum Applications semifinalist for pioneering spin‑physics approach to NMR](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1760962959613-7DD6SCSEKRGOFGYA6RCR/news-xprize.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-is-xprize-quantum-applications-semifinalist)
October 20, 2025
[ HQS is XPRIZE Quantum Applications semifinalist for pioneering spin‑physics approach to NMR ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-is-xprize-quantum-applications-semifinalist)
October 20, 2025
HQS Quantum Simulations has been selected as a semifinalist in XPRIZE Quantum Applications – a global competition designed to catalyze practical quantum‑computing algorithms that address real‑world challenges. From 133 submissions across 31 countries, 20 teams were recognized by an independent judging panel for the plausibility and quality of their ideas and for independently validated progress toward real‑world impact. Notably, half of the Semifinalists proposed genuinely new algorithms—an uncommon achievement in the field—while teams collectively target 11 societal challenges, including clean energy, climate adaptation, and public health, directly advancing the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-is-xprize-quantum-applications-semifinalist)
October 20, 2025
[ ![Launching field‑dependent NMR spin Hamiltonians for real‑world quantum algorithms](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1759827108916-E0PUOG3T8YG9YEAY817I/news+bild.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/use-case-nmr)
October 7, 2025
[ Launching field‑dependent NMR spin Hamiltonians for real‑world quantum algorithms ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/use-case-nmr)
October 7, 2025
HQS released curated, commercially relevant NMR spin Hamiltonians in Struqture format, enabling teams to benchmark algorithms on models that matter. A free 6‑month license, browser‑based access, and reference spectra via HQS Spectrum Tools make it easy to get started. [Try it yourself](https://github.com/HQSquantumsimulations/hqstage-examples): Run a NMR Use Case by making a cloud account and use our software directly in a Juypter Notebook in the browser or install locally. Find all links to tutorials, documentation, and cloud access here.  
And register for our[ webinar ](https://forms.cloud.quantumsimulations.de/159291)to help you get started and gain a deep insight into HQS Spectrum Tools.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/use-case-nmr)
October 7, 2025
[ ![HQS Announces Release of Version 1.0 of HQStage](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1752488056986-XVTTDXGDINLUS0Q47CNC/HQStage_release+1.0pptx.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-announces-release-of-version-10-of-hqstage)
July 14, 2025
[ HQS Announces Release of Version 1.0 of HQStage ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-announces-release-of-version-10-of-hqstage)
July 14, 2025
We are thrilled to announce the official release of Version 1.0 of our [HQStage](https://quantumsimulations.de/hqstage) Quantum Simulation and Quantum Computing Toolkit!   

All modules have now achieved Version 1.0, which means 
  * guaranteed stability, 
  * sleeker interfaces, 
  * and significantly improved user experience.


[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-announces-release-of-version-10-of-hqstage)
July 14, 2025
[ ![HQS Quantum Simulations supports QCOptSens in the development of a cross-compiler for automatic sensor data analysis with QML](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1744291666067-W4PI5R6XSEESAM8U4LPK/2025-03-18-Vergabe-QCOptSens-an-HQS.png?format=500w) ](https://qci.dlr.de/en/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-qcoptsens-in-the-development-of-a-cross-compiler-for-automatic-sensor-data-analysis-with-qml/)
April 10, 2025
[ HQS Quantum Simulations supports QCOptSens in the development of a cross-compiler for automatic sensor data analysis with QML ](https://qci.dlr.de/en/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-qcoptsens-in-the-development-of-a-cross-compiler-for-automatic-sensor-data-analysis-with-qml/)
April 10, 2025
HQS Quantum Simulations has been awarded to support for the sensor project QCOptSens | CrossCompiler. HQS will develop a cross-compiler for the automated transformation of a machine learning problem for optical sensing into a QML pipeline. The subcontractors are the Fraunhofer Institutes for Industrial Engineering IAO and for Manufacturing Engineering and Automation IPA.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-qcoptsens-in-the-development-of-a-cross-compiler-for-automatic-sensor-data-analysis-with-qml)
April 10, 2025
[ ![HQS Quantum Simulations receives funding from the European Innovation Council Transition Program](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1738070502110-KES1LXQ8UN9ZRWX4COQO/HQS_Managementteam_News.jpg?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-receives-funding-from-the-european-innovation-council-transition-program)
January 28, 2025
[ HQS Quantum Simulations receives funding from the European Innovation Council Transition Program ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-receives-funding-from-the-european-innovation-council-transition-program)
January 28, 2025
HQS Quantum Simulation is receiving a grant of~ 2.5 million euros from the Europe-an Innovation Council (EIC) as part of the EIC Transition Program. HQS convinced with the project “Next-Gen NMR Prediction and Analysis: A Cutting-Edge Tool for High-Precision Structure Identification” (in short HQS-NextNMR). This funding paves the way for a broader application of NMR spectroscopy and opens avenues for its use in personalized medicine.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-receives-funding-from-the-european-innovation-council-transition-program)
January 28, 2025
[ ![Year in Review](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1733905255329-WU14BMDFIO5MELXT12K2/unsplash-image-PAykYb-8Er8.jpg?format=500w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/year-in-review-24)
December 12, 2024
[ Year in Review ](https://quantumsimulations.de/year-in-review-24)
December 12, 2024
As we approach the end of 2024, we reflect with gratitude on an incredible year shaped by shared achievements and meaningful collaborations. Together with our partners and customers, we’ve been fortunate to work on fascinating commissions and pioneering research projects that push the boundaries of innovation.
**Thank you for being an integral part of this journey. We invite you to explore the key milestones and highlights of this year below, showcasing the incredible projects we’ve accomplished together.**
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/year-in-review)
December 12, 2024
[ ![Project QSolid: Quantum Computer Demonstrator in Operation](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1731414803925-X36R9TE60MQVBQIN64O5/unsplash-image-Xaanw0s0pMk.jpg?format=500w) ](https://www.fz-juelich.de/en/news/archive/press-release/2024/project-qsolid-quantum-computer-demonstrator-in-operation)
November 12, 2024
[ Project QSolid: Quantum Computer Demonstrator in Operation ](https://www.fz-juelich.de/en/news/archive/press-release/2024/project-qsolid-quantum-computer-demonstrator-in-operation)
November 12, 2024
In the QSolid collaborative project, Forschungszentrum Jülich and its partners have started operating the first prototype of a quantum computer with optimised qubit quality. It forms the basis of a future quantum computer being developed in Germany using superconducting qubits, which will be able to perform complex calculations for industry and research.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/project-qsolid-quantum-computer-demonstrator-in-operation)
November 12, 2024
[ ![HQS Quantum Simulations supports QuantiCoM | MatQML in the development of a QML-based metamodel for the prediction of material properties](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1729507058564-MUQEMI0PG587M7K19ZQZ/2024-10-16+Vergabe+QuantiCoM+MatQML+an+HQS.png?format=500w) ](https://qci.dlr.de/en/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-quanticom-matqml/)
October 21, 2024
[ HQS Quantum Simulations supports QuantiCoM | MatQML in the development of a QML-based metamodel for the prediction of material properties ](https://qci.dlr.de/en/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-quanticom-matqml/)
October 21, 2024
The DLR QCI put out a call for tenders to find support for the QuantiCoM materials research project at the DLR Institute of Materials Research: The contract has now been awarded to HQS Quantum Simulations.   
  
The QuantiCoM project aims to enhance materials research by integrating quantum machine learning (QML) with traditional data-based methods to predict and optimize the properties of alloys. 
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-supports-quanticom-matqml-in-the-development-of-a-qml-based-metamodel-for-the-prediction-of-material-properties)
October 21, 2024
[ ![Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1728975167339-SX8JVRZ69782AZSOM1Z7/news_webinar_november.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-ai-assistance-for-model-building)
October 15, 2024
[ Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-ai-assistance-for-model-building)
October 15, 2024
Join our informative webinar “Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building”, where we will introduce the innovative HQS Modeling Assistant.
**Webinar: Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building**
**Date: Wednesday, November 13, 2024  
Time: 15:30 – 17:00 CET**
Artificial intelligence will make our work much easier. Minimizing errors and saving time are crucial factors to improve research, development and analysis in many areas. As a provider of quantum simulation software, we have developed an assistant that makes it easier for engineers and scientists to work with scientific software.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-ai-assistance-for-model-building)
October 15, 2024
[ ![Discover the Solution to the Barren Plateau Problem: Join our free HQStage Webinar on Non-Unitary Gates in Quantum Algorithms!](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1725364430911-4YNSITJ028UC2AC8I4DV/news_webinar_october_v2.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-on-non-unitary-gates-in-quantum-algorithms)
September 3, 2024
[ Discover the Solution to the Barren Plateau Problem: Join our free HQStage Webinar on Non-Unitary Gates in Quantum Algorithms! ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-on-non-unitary-gates-in-quantum-algorithms)
September 3, 2024
HQS is excited to announce our upcoming webinar on **"Non-Unitary Gates in Quantum Algorithms: Overcoming the Barren Plateau Problem."** This insightful webinar will delve into the theoretical foundation behind our innovative software, HQStage, and explore how non-unitary gates can effectively tackle the challenges of optimizing parameterized quantum circuits.
The barren plateau problem has long been a major challenge in quantum machine learning and optimization. At HQS, we have implemented a groundbreaking approach to overcome this obstacle using our powerful HQStage software. By leveraging non-unitary gates, we have reshaped the training landscape, enabling more effective convergence and unlocking the full potential of quantum simulation and other applications for quantum computers.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/webinar-on-non-unitary-gates-in-quantum-algorithms)
September 3, 2024
[ ![HQS Quantum Simulations launches HQS Tasks for computationally intensive applications](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1721983518728-LV6OWKR6Z9OQEULMXKJA/HQS%2BTasks%2Bvorschaubild.jpg?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-launches-hqs-tasks-for-computationally-intensive-applications)
July 25, 2024
[ HQS Quantum Simulations launches HQS Tasks for computationally intensive applications ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-launches-hqs-tasks-for-computationally-intensive-applications)
July 25, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations is pleased to announce the release of HQS Tasks: an innovative software module of the HQStage toolkit for exploiting large amounts of computational resources with minimal effort.   
“HQS Tasks opens up a world of computing resources without having to worry about setup," says Sebastian Lehmann, head of development for HQS Tasks. “HQS Tasks users do not need their own computing resources. They can specify the hardware resources they need to run their tasks, although HQS provides optimal default values.”  
One of the key features of HQS Tasks is its seamless integration with Python, allowing users to stay in their favorite Jupyter notebook environment while accessing our tasks. The principle of detached sending and retrieving of results ensures that users can comfortably continue their workflow at their own pace.   

[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-quantum-simulations-launches-hqs-tasks-for-computationally-intensive-applications)
July 25, 2024
[ ![HQS Introduces an Update to the Open-Source Tool Active Space Finder, Collaborating with Covestro](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1720428016850-M98NE4QC8ZN8JEB387Z6/ASF_HQS.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/update-active-space-finder-collaborating-with-covestro)
July 8, 2024
[ HQS Introduces an Update to the Open-Source Tool Active Space Finder, Collaborating with Covestro ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/update-active-space-finder-collaborating-with-covestro)
July 8, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations (HQS) announces the release of an update to its open-source tool, the Active Space Finder (ASF). Developed in collaboration with Covestro, a global player in the field of polymer materials, this update aims to enhance chemical simulations by simplifying the selection of active spaces in molecules.
Selecting the appropriate set of active orbitals can be a complex task that requires significant expertise. Recognizing this challenge, HQS and Covestro have been working together since 2020 to develop a tool that makes such calculations easier and more accessible to both expert and non-expert users. The initial version of the ASF has been available on GitHub since 2021, and users can now download the improved version.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/update-active-space-finder-collaborating-with-covestro)
July 8, 2024
[ ![HQStage Webinar Series: Explore a New Era of Simulation Technology](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1719316334256-UN7G28U9V861IV7VMON9/news.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/hqstage-webinar-series)
June 19, 2024
[ HQStage Webinar Series: Explore a New Era of Simulation Technology ](https://quantumsimulations.de/hqstage-webinar-series)
June 19, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations offers a comprehensive webinar series introducing the newly launched HQStage software toolkit, exploring current applications in quantum computing. The HQStage webinar series starts with one-hour sessions on installation, feature exploration, and practical use cases. We also cover features from scientific papers on NMR and algorithmic cooling. This is followed by in-depth webinars on advanced topics like non-unitary gates in quantum algorithms and AI for quantum simulation. Find out more about joining these free webinars. 
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqstage-webinar-series)
June 19, 2024
[ ![HQS introduces HQStage, a modular quantum simulation and quantum computing toolkit](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1714472130619-K6TTNUO5DGS5B961YXMW/HQStage.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-introduces-hqstage)
April 30, 2024
[ HQS introduces HQStage, a modular quantum simulation and quantum computing toolkit ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-introduces-hqstage)
April 30, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations proudly presents HQStage, a powerful modular cloud-supported toolkit designed to meet the simulation needs of developers and researchers in quantum physics and chemistry. HQStage offers 10 powerful simulation modules, intuitive management tools and the flexibility to combine local and cloud computing. 
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/hqs-introduces-hqstage)
April 30, 2024
[ ![Exploring Open-Quantum Systems and Noise Characteristics - In Collaboration with the LRZ, HQS Quantum Simulations presents the HQS Noise App at a Workshop](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1709199442154-FVROAIUZ76E7OAQAA3V6/LRZ_workshop.jpg?format=500w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/exploring-open-quantum-systems-and-noise-characteristics-in-collaboration-with-the-lrz-hqs-quantum-simulations-presents-the-hqs-noise-app-at-a-workshop)
March 21, 2024
[ Exploring Open-Quantum Systems and Noise Characteristics - In Collaboration with the LRZ, HQS Quantum Simulations presents the HQS Noise App at a Workshop ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/exploring-open-quantum-systems-and-noise-characteristics-in-collaboration-with-the-lrz-hqs-quantum-simulations-presents-the-hqs-noise-app-at-a-workshop)
March 21, 2024
The Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ) has recently installed the HQS Noise App from HQS Quantum Simulations, a significant milestone in their collaborative efforts to advance quantum computing technology. This installation is a key component of the Q-Exa project, where LRZ and HQS aim to explore the intricate details of quantum computing and accelerate the development of quantum computing applications for various companies and researchers. To meet the objectives of the Q-Exa project, the HQS Noise App underwent significant enhancements. These enhancements were carefully designed to align the app's capabilities with the project's goals. After rigorous development and testing, the final version of the app has been successfully delivered. LRZ and HQS are delighted to announce a joint workshop to showcase the latest iteration of the HQS Noise App.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/exploring-open-quantum-systems-and-noise-characteristics-in-collaboration-with-the-lrz-hqs-quantum-simulations-presents-the-hqs-noise-app-at-a-workshop)
March 21, 2024
[ ![Final phase of the QRydDemo project in collaboration with the University of Stuttgart - Development of a compiler for Rydberg atom-based quantum computers](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1683713126453-2SWBAXM97Z9K5QON0C2W/Software_news.png?format=300w) ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/final-phase-of-the-qryddemo-project)
February 26, 2024
[ Final phase of the QRydDemo project in collaboration with the University of Stuttgart - Development of a compiler for Rydberg atom-based quantum computers ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/final-phase-of-the-qryddemo-project)
February 26, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations (HQS), a quantum startup from Karlsruhe, is supporting the QRydDemo project on behalf of the University of Stuttgart by developing a special compiler. The QRydDemo project is building a quantum computer based on neutral atoms. The compiler developed by HQS is specifically tailored to the unique features of this quantum computer. HQS will continue its support for the project, initiated in 2021 and scheduled to run until January 2025, and will improve the compiler further.
[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/final-phase-of-the-qryddemo-project)
February 26, 2024

January 16, 2024
As part of the AutoQML project on behalf of Fraunhofer IPA, HQS has developed a robust software library, the AutoSelectionBackend, which enables the automatic transpilation of quantum machine learning circuits. To simplify and optimize the development of QML solutions in an industrial context, the AutoQML project, led by Fraunhofer IAO, aims to integrate quantum components into the field of automated machine learning (AutoML). The library has been successfully integrated into the AutoQML framework and is now available as an open-source solution on the PlanQK platform.

HQS Quantum Simulations provides setups for simulating chemical systems such as metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) on quantum computers on behalf of Fraunhofer IAF and ICT. December 5, 2023
HQS Quantum Simulations GmbH is proud to announce the delivery of a specialized quantum simulation software package to the DLR Institute. The order was placed as part of the DLR Quantum Computing Initiative. In particular, the simulation of material properties and the analysis of material dynamics on quantum computers is of great interest to industry. This requires the development of customized compilation strategies and quantum algorithms in hardware-software co-design for current quantum computers of the NISQ era. Successful collaboration on this challenge represents a major advance in the benefits of quantum technology for industrial end users.

July 24, 2023
HQS Quantum Simulations, a leading provider of quantum software solutions, is thrilled to announce the release of Qoqo for Braket, an innovative interface that seamlessly converts quantum circuits of the HQS Quantum Computing toolkit Qoqo to Amazon Braket circuits, enabling their execution on QPUs and simulators available on Amazon Braket.

July 19, 2023
[ SEEQC System Red Successfully runs HQS Quantum Simulations Algorithm ](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/seeqc-system-red-successfully-runs-hqs-quantum-simulations-algorithm)
July 19, 2023
First full-stack quantum computer able to run an algorithm developed by HQS Quantum Simulations, demonstrating potential for near-term quantum advantage.   
SEEQC, the digital quantum computing company, today announced with HQS Quantum Simulations that it has successfully run an algorithm on its SEEQC System Red, the company’s full-stack quantum computing system, that shows great potential to achieve commercially useful quantum advantage in the near term. SEEQCs high-quality system characterization has allowed HQS Quantum Simulations to test, for the first time on real hardware, their unique approach to perform quantum simulation on noisy quantum computers. This is an important third-party validation for SEEQC and a testament to the high quality and commercially stable performance of SEEQC Red’s platform — the company’s first-generation reference class quantum computer system.  

[Read more →](https://quantumsimulations.de/news/seeqc-system-red-successfully-runs-hqs-quantum-simulations-algorithm)

June 19, 2023
HQS Quantum Simulations, Europe’s leading startup for complex quantum simulations, receives further capital injection and strengthens its management team. The TRUMPF Group’s corporate venture capital unit, Trumpf Venture II GmbH, joins the company as a new one-million-euro investor. HQS Quantum Simulations had already closed on a Series A financing round last year. Michael Bolle has also been part of the Advisory Board since then. The former CTO and CDO of Robert Bosch GmbH brings along years of experience as a technology expert and leading manager. He is primarily dedicated in his new role to the technological development of HQS Quantum Simulations’ software.

May 23, 2023
Quantum software company HQS Quantum Simulations has been commissioned by BASF to develop use cases of chemistry and materials simulation on quantum computers for the emerging QuCUN platform. QuCUN is a joint project funded by the German Federal Ministry of Education and Research, for which the QAR-Lab of the Ludwig-Maximilians-University Munich, Aqarios, SAP, and BASF have joined forces in a consortium. The project aims to provide an online platform that serves as an ecosystem for developers and users of quantum computing.

# Upcoming Events
Events
[ ![Can I use Benchtop NMR in my lab? ](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5f34fa00fe2696063683642a/1779101752181-ZPS1TO5BIDU0G17ATSI2/webinar%2Bbenchtop%2Bnmr.png?format=300w) Jun 22 ](https://quantumsimulations.de/events/can-i-use-benchtop-nmr-in-my-lab)
[ Can I use Benchtop NMR in my lab? ](https://quantumsimulations.de/events/can-i-use-benchtop-nmr-in-my-lab)
Discover how benchtop NMR can streamline your analytical workflows!
Join this interactive workshop to explore where benchtop NMR truly adds value in routine lab operations — and how tools like HQSpectrum, ChemisTwin® (Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany), and the QM-125 (Q Magnetics) can help you move from molecular structure to decision-ready spectral insights with minimal effort. Whether you’re evaluating benchtop NMR for your lab or looking to get more out of it, this free webinar offers practical guidance you can apply right away.

Latest News
February 27, 2026
HQS launches HQSpectrum — full release of its NMR spectrum prediction and analysis software
February 27, 2026

HQS Quantum Simulations introduces HQSpectrum, the first full version of its software for predicting and analyzing Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectra, designed to reduce manual effort in NMR analysis and to improve traceability. Users can preprocess experimental spectra, compare them against computed spectra, adjust results interactively, and export documentation-ready reports.

Read more →
February 27, 2026
December 15, 2025
Year in Review 2025: Accelerating Quantum‑Powered Spectroscopy at HQS
December 15, 2025

2025 got off to a spectacular start for HQS Quantum Simulations: the year began with our EIC Transition success, setting the tone for twelve months focused on scaling impact, expanding ready‑to‑use workflows, and strengthening collaborations that bring quantum methods for spectroscopy closer to real‑world use.

Read more →
December 15, 2025
December 11, 2025
Dr Nicolas Vogt Appointed Chief Innovation Officer at HQS
December 11, 2025

HQS Quantum Simulations is pleased to announce that its management team has grown. Dr Nicolas Vogt, previously Head of Quantum Computing, has been appointed Chief Innovation Officer.
In his new role, Dr Vogt will focus on exploring new application areas and developing prototypes that will later be integrated into the HQSpectrum product line.

The position is designed to sit at the intersection of cutting edge research and product development, ensuring that novel concepts in spectroscopy and quantum technologies are translated quickly into solutions for customers.

Read more →
December 11, 2025
November 12, 2025
HQS releases Electron Spectroscopy Use Cases
November 12, 2025

Quantum simulation is rapidly reshaping electron spectroscopy. By mapping complex many-body problems onto compact quantum models, researchers can estimate excitation energies and singlet–triplet gaps using smaller circuit footprints, bringing previously computationally challenging observables within the reach of today's and tomorrow's quantum hardware.

HQS Quantum Simulations is making new Electron Spectroscopy use cases available on the HQS Cloud together with a complimentary 6‑month license of the HQStage module HQS Quantum Solver for evaluation.

Read more →
November 12, 2025
October 20, 2025
HQS is XPRIZE Quantum Applications semifinalist for pioneering spin‑physics approach to NMR
October 20, 2025

HQS Quantum Simulations has been selected as a semifinalist in XPRIZE Quantum Applications – a global competition designed to catalyze practical quantum‑computing algorithms that address real‑world challenges. From 133 submissions across 31 countries, 20 teams were recognized by an independent judging panel for the plausibility and quality of their ideas and for independently validated progress toward real‑world impact. Notably, half of the Semifinalists proposed genuinely new algorithms—an uncommon achievement in the field—while teams collectively target 11 societal challenges, including clean energy, climate adaptation, and public health, directly advancing the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Read more →
October 20, 2025
October 7, 2025
Launching field‑dependent NMR spin Hamiltonians for real‑world quantum algorithms
October 7, 2025

HQS released curated, commercially relevant NMR spin Hamiltonians in Struqture format, enabling teams to benchmark algorithms on models that matter. A free 6‑month license, browser‑based access, and reference spectra via HQS Spectrum Tools make it easy to get started. Try it yourself: Run a NMR Use Case by making a cloud account and use our software directly in a Juypter Notebook in the browser or install locally. Find all links to tutorials, documentation, and cloud access here.
And register for our webinar to help you get started and gain a deep insight into HQS Spectrum Tools.

Read more →
October 7, 2025
July 14, 2025
HQS Announces Release of Version 1.0 of HQStage
July 14, 2025

We are thrilled to announce the official release of Version 1.0 of our HQStage Quantum Simulation and Quantum Computing Toolkit!

All modules have now achieved Version 1.0, which means

  • guaranteed stability,

  • sleeker interfaces,

  • and significantly improved user experience.

HQS Quantum Simulations supports QCOptSens in the development of a cross-compiler for automatic sensor data analysis with QML
April 10, 2025

HQS Quantum Simulations has been awarded to support for the sensor project QCOptSens | CrossCompiler. HQS will develop a cross-compiler for the automated transformation of a machine learning problem for optical sensing into a QML pipeline. The subcontractors are the Fraunhofer Institutes for Industrial Engineering IAO and for Manufacturing Engineering and Automation IPA.

January 28, 2025
HQS Quantum Simulations receives funding from the European Innovation Council Transition Program
January 28, 2025

HQS Quantum Simulation is receiving a grant of~ 2.5 million euros from the Europe-an Innovation Council (EIC) as part of the EIC Transition Program. HQS convinced with the project “Next-Gen NMR Prediction and Analysis: A Cutting-Edge Tool for High-Precision Structure Identification” (in short HQS-NextNMR). This funding paves the way for a broader application of NMR spectroscopy and opens avenues for its use in personalized medicine.

Read more →

December 12, 2024
Year in Review
December 12, 2024

As we approach the end of 2024, we reflect with gratitude on an incredible year shaped by shared achievements and meaningful collaborations. Together with our partners and customers, we’ve been fortunate to work on fascinating commissions and pioneering research projects that push the boundaries of innovation.

Thank you for being an integral part of this journey. We invite you to explore the key milestones and highlights of this year below, showcasing the incredible projects we’ve accomplished together.

Read more →
December 12, 2024
November 12, 2024
Project QSolid: Quantum Computer Demonstrator in Operation
November 12, 2024

In the QSolid collaborative project, Forschungszentrum Jülich and its partners have started operating the first prototype of a quantum computer with optimised qubit quality. It forms the basis of a future quantum computer being developed in Germany using superconducting qubits, which will be able to perform complex calculations for industry and research.

Read more →
November 12, 2024
October 21, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations supports QuantiCoM | MatQML in the development of a QML-based metamodel for the prediction of material properties
October 21, 2024

The DLR QCI put out a call for tenders to find support for the QuantiCoM materials research project at the DLR Institute of Materials Research: The contract has now been awarded to HQS Quantum Simulations.

The QuantiCoM project aims to enhance materials research by integrating quantum machine learning (QML) with traditional data-based methods to predict and optimize the properties of alloys.

Read more →
October 21, 2024
October 15, 2024
Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building
October 15, 2024

Join our informative webinar “Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building”, where we will introduce the innovative HQS Modeling Assistant.

Webinar: Maximizing the Use for Quantum Simulation through AI Assistance for Model Building

Date: Wednesday, November 13, 2024
Time: 15:30 – 17:00 CET

Artificial intelligence will make our work much easier. Minimizing errors and saving time are crucial factors to improve research, development and analysis in many areas. As a provider of quantum simulation software, we have developed an assistant that makes it easier for engineers and scientists to work with scientific software.

Read more →
October 15, 2024
September 3, 2024
Discover the Solution to the Barren Plateau Problem: Join our free HQStage Webinar on Non-Unitary Gates in Quantum Algorithms!
September 3, 2024

HQS is excited to announce our upcoming webinar on "Non-Unitary Gates in Quantum Algorithms: Overcoming the Barren Plateau Problem." This insightful webinar will delve into the theoretical foundation behind our innovative software, HQStage, and explore how non-unitary gates can effectively tackle the challenges of optimizing parameterized quantum circuits.

The barren plateau problem has long been a major challenge in quantum machine learning and optimization. At HQS, we have implemented a groundbreaking approach to overcome this obstacle using our powerful HQStage software. By leveraging non-unitary gates, we have reshaped the training landscape, enabling more effective convergence and unlocking the full potential of quantum simulation and other applications for quantum computers.

Read more →
September 3, 2024
July 25, 2024
HQS Quantum Simulations launches HQS Tasks for computationally intensive applications
July 25, 2024

HQS Quantum Simulations is pleased to announce the release of HQS Tasks: an innovative software module of the HQStage toolkit for exploiting large amounts of computational resources with minimal effort.
“HQS Tasks opens up a world of computing resources without having to worry about setup," says Sebastian Lehmann, head of development for HQS Tasks. “HQS Tasks users do not need their own computing resources. They can specify the hardware resources they need to run their tasks, although HQS provides optimal default values.”
One of the key features of HQS Tasks is its seamless integration with Python, allowing users to stay in their favorite Jupyter notebook environment while accessing our tasks. The principle of detached sending and retrieving of results ensures that users can comfortably continue their workflow at their own pace.

Read more →
July 25, 2024
July 8, 2024
HQS Introduces an Update to the Open-Source Tool Active Space Finder, Collaborating with Covestro
July 8, 2024

HQS Quantum Simulations (HQS) announces the release of an update to its open-source tool, the Active Space Finder (ASF). Developed in collaboration with Covestro, a global player in the field of polymer materials, this update aims to enhance chemical simulations by simplifying the selection of active spaces in molecules.

Selecting the appropriate set of active orbitals can be a complex task that requires significant expertise. Recognizing this challenge, HQS and Covestro have been working together since 2020 to develop a tool that makes such calculations easier and more accessible to both expert and non-expert users. The initial version of the ASF has been available on GitHub since 2021, and users can now download the improved version.

Read more →
July 8, 2024
June 19, 2024
HQStage Webinar Series: Explore a New Era of Simulation Technology
June 19, 2024

HQS Quantum Simulations offers a comprehensive webinar series introducing the newly launched HQStage software toolkit, exploring current applications in quantum computing. The HQStage webinar series starts with one-hour sessions on installation, feature exploration, and practical use cases. We also cover features from scientific papers on NMR and algorithmic cooling. This is followed by in-depth webinars on advanced topics like non-unitary gates in quantum algorithms and AI for quantum simulation. Find out more about joining these free webinars.

Read more →
June 19, 2024
April 30, 2024
HQS introduces HQStage, a modular quantum simulation and quantum computing toolkit
April 30, 2024

HQS Quantum Simulations proudly presents HQStage, a powerful modular cloud-supported toolkit designed to meet the simulation needs of developers and researchers in quantum physics and chemistry. HQStage offers 10 powerful simulation modules, intuitive management tools and the flexibility to combine local and cloud computing.

Read more →

March 21, 2024
Exploring Open-Quantum Systems and Noise Characteristics - In Collaboration with the LRZ, HQS Quantum Simulations presents the HQS Noise App at a Workshop
March 21, 2024

The Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ) has recently installed the HQS Noise App from HQS Quantum Simulations, a significant milestone in their collaborative efforts to advance quantum computing technology. This installation is a key component of the Q-Exa project, where LRZ and HQS aim to explore the intricate details of quantum computing and accelerate the development of quantum computing applications for various companies and researchers. To meet the objectives of the Q-Exa project, the HQS Noise App underwent significant enhancements. These enhancements were carefully designed to align the app's capabilities with the project's goals. After rigorous development and testing, the final version of the app has been successfully delivered. LRZ and HQS are delighted to announce a joint workshop to showcase the latest iteration of the HQS Noise App.

Read more →

February 26, 2024
Final phase of the QRydDemo project in collaboration with the University of Stuttgart - Development of a compiler for Rydberg atom-based quantum computers
February 26, 2024

HQS Quantum Simulations (HQS), a quantum startup from Karlsruhe, is supporting the QRydDemo project on behalf of the University of Stuttgart by developing a special compiler. The QRydDemo project is building a quantum computer based on neutral atoms. The compiler developed by HQS is specifically tailored to the unique features of this quantum computer. HQS will continue its support for the project, initiated in 2021 and scheduled to run until January 2025, and will improve the compiler further.

Read more →

As part of the AutoQML project on behalf of Fraunhofer IPA, HQS has developed a robust software library, the AutoSelectionBackend, which enables the automatic transpilation of quantum machine learning circuits. To simplify and optimize the development of QML solutions in an industrial context, the AutoQML project, led by Fraunhofer IAO, aims to integrate quantum components into the field of automated machine learning (AutoML). The library has been successfully integrated into the AutoQML framework and is now available as an open-source solution on the PlanQK platform.

December 5, 2023

HQS Quantum Simulations GmbH is proud to announce the delivery of a specialized quantum simulation software package to the DLR Institute. The order was placed as part of the DLR Quantum Computing Initiative. In particular, the simulation of material properties and the analysis of material dynamics on quantum computers is of great interest to industry. This requires the development of customized compilation strategies and quantum algorithms in hardware-software co-design for current quantum computers of the NISQ era. Successful collaboration on this challenge represents a major advance in the benefits of quantum technology for industrial end users.

July 19, 2023
SEEQC System Red Successfully runs HQS Quantum Simulations Algorithm
July 19, 2023

First full-stack quantum computer able to run an algorithm developed by HQS Quantum Simulations, demonstrating potential for near-term quantum advantage.
SEEQC, the digital quantum computing company, today announced with HQS Quantum Simulations that it has successfully run an algorithm on its SEEQC System Red, the company’s full-stack quantum computing system, that shows great potential to achieve commercially useful quantum advantage in the near term. SEEQCs high-quality system characterization has allowed HQS Quantum Simulations to test, for the first time on real hardware, their unique approach to perform quantum simulation on noisy quantum computers. This is an important third-party validation for SEEQC and a testament to the high quality and commercially stable performance of SEEQC Red’s platform — the company’s first-generation reference class quantum computer system.

HQS Quantum Simulations drives quantum revolution with fresh capital and strengthened team
June 19, 2023

HQS Quantum Simulations, Europe’s leading startup for complex quantum simulations, receives further capital injection and strengthens its management team. The TRUMPF Group’s corporate venture capital unit, Trumpf Venture II GmbH, joins the company as a new one-million-euro investor. HQS Quantum Simulations had already closed on a Series A financing round last year. Michael Bolle has also been part of the Advisory Board since then. The former CTO and CDO of Robert Bosch GmbH brings along years of experience as a technology expert and leading manager. He is primarily dedicated in his new role to the technological development of HQS Quantum Simulations’ software.

May 23, 2023
Materials science applications for quantum computing on the QuCUN platform - HQS receives an order from BASF
May 23, 2023

Quantum software company HQS Quantum Simulations has been commissioned by BASF to develop use cases of chemistry and materials simulation on quantum computers for the emerging QuCUN platform. QuCUN is a joint project funded by the German Federal Ministry of Education and Research, for which the QAR-Lab of the Ludwig-Maximilians-University Munich, Aqarios, SAP, and BASF have joined forces in a consortium. The project aims to provide an online platform that serves as an ecosystem for developers and users of quantum computing.
